text:
This article is about SEELE from Neon Genesis Evangelion. To see the version from Rebuild of Evangelion, see SEELE.

 is a secret and mysterious organization with significant influence over world governments and many international institutions in the Neon Genesis Evangelion franchise.

Profile

SEELE strives to accomplish the Human Instrumentality Project—the goal of uniting the human race as one, thereby ending all wars and individual struggles of mankind.

The Seed of Adam (the progenitor of the Angels) was the first to arrive on Earth. However, due to the later arrival of the Seed of Lilith (the progenitor of humanity), Adam's seed was inactivated by its respective Spear of Longinus. SEELE therefore views humanity as the false successors of Earth and the Angels as the true successors—the sons of God. Because of this, humanity is seen as bearing the "Original Sin," having come into existence when it should not have.

SEELE's plan is to destroy the Angels, then unite all of humanity and ultimately merge with both Lilith and Adam, becoming one with God—the next stage of existence. SEELE's original plan to accomplish this was to have Kaworu, the Seventeenth Angel and carrier of Adam's soul, merge with Lilith, thereby triggering the Third Impact. With the assistance of thirteen Evangelion units, this would initiate the Human Instrumentality Project.

However, the loss of Eva Units 03 and 04, along with delays in the construction and deployment of Units 05 and 06, led SEELE to construct the Mass Production Evangelions, which were operated using Kaworu Dummy Plugs.

Kaworu’s death led SEELE to conclude that he was not the "successor of life" after all. This was further justified by the Dead Sea Scrolls, as they contained no prophecy or mention of these events. From that point on, SEELE agreed not to rely on the power of the Angels to accomplish their goals.

The only named member of SEELE is Keel Lorenz (original German spelling: Lorenz Kiel), along with fourteen other unidentified men (four of whom are representatives of the Instrumentality Committee). All other SEELE-affiliated individuals and groups hold no official power within the organization. It is speculated that they may be some form of A.I. similar to the Magi, or that they are human beings like Kaworu Nagisa or Rei Ayanami, with ties to Adam and Lilith.

SEELE operates NERV under the name of the Human Instrumentality Committee, a body consisting of five SEELE members, including Keel Lorenz, who officially represents the United Nations. During Instrumentality Committee meetings, each member is associated with a different color and nation. Evangelion Chronicle 7, pg. 11-13 However, in official SEELE meetings, the participants are represented by large stone monoliths labeled “SEELE — Sound Only,” rather than by images of the actual members.

It is presumed that Keel Lorenz, designated as SEELE 01, is the organization’s leader, as he speaks more frequently and often on behalf of the entire group. The number of SEELE members shown in meetings varies: typically twelve, sometimes as many as fifteen, and as few as seven.

Trivia

"SEELE" [/ˈzeːlə/] as written is literal German for "soul". The pronunciation can be found on: http://www.duden.de/rechtschreibung/Seele 

The SEELE logo may very well be a reference to the Revelation 5 https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation%205&version=ESV in the ', which describes the , and a seven eyed Lamb; this exact layout of three eyes on the left side, four on the right, can be found in a classical artistic depictions of this Lamb. ' is rife with apocalyptic imagery, but it's commonly believed among Protestants that the seventh and final seal represents the Rapture, where those deemed worthy would rise into heaven to be with God while those deemed unworthy would die below on Earth. This concept closely mirrors SEELE's goals.
The monoliths seen during SEELE meetings heavily resemble that of in Stanley Kubrick's '.
